Control panel

App Command Centre

Update the figures the calculator uses — brackets, fees, grants, surcharges and first-home thresholds — then publish. No code required. Use the live preview on the right to sanity-check a change before you export.

Publishing & preview
Site tools

Calculators shown on the site

Tick the calculators you want visible on the public page. Unticked ones are hidden from the tabs entirely.

Captured leads (demo · masked)

Visitors who emailed themselves results land here. In this demo, names and emails are masked for privacy — the live version stores full contact details and the complete scenario for your follow-up.

Edit rates

Pick a state, change the numbers. Rates are entered as percentages; thresholds and fees as dollars.

Fees, grants & thresholds

Settings for the selected state — these feed the same preview and calculations as the schedule.

Appearance

Match the calculator to the broker's website — pick a starter theme, then fine-tune any colour or font. Changes apply live, save automatically, and travel with your exported settings (appearance in rates.json).

Save this look

Tune the colours above, then Publish Site to save them to this site. To save a reusable, named look for the demo site (/demo), sign in as the platform admin and use the Demo themes tab — those are the ones visitors can switch between.

A theme file includes a logo URL — we can build a paid custom theme you simply load on.

Landing pages

Build SEO-optimised pages that automatically use your theme colours and fonts, then add them to the site's top menu or footer. Each page gets its own clean URL slug. Published pages are included in sitemap.xml — after publishing, go to Settings \u2192 Search & indexing and click Regenerate sitemap.

Edit page

Font colours

Optional colours for this landing page only. Leave blank to use the site theme.

AI draft ready — review before it fills SEO fields and attaches Marketplace FAQ (Unique)
Page image
Call-to-action buttons

Add marketplace apps to this landing page. Shared uses live homepage content. Unique keeps a per-page copy — choose Unique then click Use homepage content to copy styling, images, and text from the homepage before editing for this page.

Choose an app from the list first.

Page layout — position on page

Drag sections and Page content into the order you want on this landing page — e.g. hero, article, quote form.

No apps on this page yet — add one above, or leave empty for a text-only article page.

Edit app

Contact & brokers

Add the broker(s) behind this site. One broker shows a single bio with an enquiry form; multiple brokers show a team page where visitors choose who to talk to. Every enquiry is captured into Leads (name, phone & email required).

Brokers

Logo & header

Control the masthead on your calculator page. Turn any element off if you're embedding inside a site that already has its own branding and you want it stripped back.

PNG/SVG/JPG — stored with your settings.
Header preview

Users & access

Add the people who can sign in, and choose what each can see. In this demo, accounts are stored locally so you can try the access levels — real sign-in security arrives with the hosted backend.

Super admin

Full access — rates, leads, appearance, landing pages, contact, logo and users.

Broker

Edit their own details & broker page, edit rates, and see leads. No access to users.

Leads only

Sees the leads panel and nothing else — ideal for a front-desk or assistant.

People
Add a user

Demo themes

Looks a prospect can try on your demo site (/demo). Design a look in the Appearance tab, then save it here with the client's name and choose whether it shows. Visitors apply them live on the demo — nothing is saved to their browser or yours.

Add the current look

Switch to Appearance, build the look, then come back here and save — it captures whatever is set there now.

On the demo

Shared partner themes

Save the design of the site you're editing as a theme every partner can apply to their own demos. Build the look you want on any site, then share it — partners see it in their theme picker.

Captures the current site's full design. Open a site first, design it, then share.

Shared with partners

Paste rates JSON

Why conversion rate matters

Conversion rate is the share of visitors who become enquiries. For trade and service websites, industry benchmarks typically look like this:

  • 10%+ — excellent: your site is turning traffic into leads very effectively
  • 5–9% — good: solid performance; small tweaks can lift results further
  • 2–4% — average: typical for many local trade sites
  • Below 2% — below average: worth reviewing your offer, calls-to-action, and page speed

Tracking this number helps you spot whether more visitors are actually reaching out — not just browsing. A healthy conversion rate means your site is doing its job between ad spend, SEO, and word of mouth.
